title: The One With The Cheap Wedding Dress

jokes.text: - You're into swing music? I used to go all over town listening to bands. - Chandler? - Gap commercial.
jokes.setup: Ross claims he used to go all over town listening to swing bands, suggesting musical sophistication
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals Ross only knows swing from a Gap commercial
jokes.explanation: Sharp deflation of Ross's pretensions with perfect economy. Very character-specific - only Chandler would deliver this kind of cutting observation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I used to go all over town listening to swing bands. I was really into it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, the Gap commercial.

jokes.text: - What is your job? - Staying out of the way.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Chandler what his job is in wedding planning
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest admission that his role is just staying out of the way
jokes.explanation: Relatable joke about male roles in wedding planning. Good self-aware humor from Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So what is your job in all of this wedding planning?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: My job? To stay out of the way.

jokes.text: - There were only 12. - Oh, yeah, I added three.
jokes.setup: Monica corrects Ross about there being 15 appetizers when there were only 12
jokes.punchline: Ross casually admits he just added three more to the list
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about Ross's casual approach to the appetizer selection process.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: There were only 12 appetizers, not 15.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well I just added three more to the list.

jokes.text: Being engaged, I'm not nervous talking to pretty girls anymore.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why he's confident about talking to the attractive new neighbor
jokes.punchline: The irony that being engaged would make him less nervous (when it should make talking to pretty girls irrelevant)
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Chandler's rationalization, but not particularly sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Being engaged actually makes me less nervous talking to pretty girls. I mean, I'm like, 'Oh, she's attractive, but I'm taken, so... no pressure!'

jokes.text: This street is the first in the city to have an underground sewer system. Before that, sewage and waste would just flow right down the street. Sometimes ankle-deep.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to impress Kristen by sharing the street's 'interesting history'
jokes.punchline: He chooses the most unromantic, disgusting historical fact possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - his nerdy enthusiasm leading to completely inappropriate romantic conversation. Great cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, this street has a really interesting history. Back in 1987, there was a sewage backup that lasted for three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Kristen
dialogue.line: That's... that's the least romantic thing I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh! Or did you know that a hot dog cart exploded on this corner in 1992?

jokes.text: Smooth.
jokes.setup: Ross has just given a gross history lesson about sewage to an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry, sarcastic one-word commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. Classic Chandler straight-man reaction to Ross's social awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Smooth.

jokes.text: Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler are both trying to be gentlemanly, insisting the other go first
jokes.punchline: Their politeness leads to a physical mishap (collision or dropped items)
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. Predictable outcome from the 'after you' routine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, you go first.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I insist, you go first.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Chandler, please, after you.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, I'm not going anywhere until you go.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not moving until you move.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Fine, okay, on three. One... two...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, what are we doing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know!

dialogue.speaker: Both
dialogue.line: [They both lunge forward simultaneously and crash into each other, sending papers and coffee flying]

jokes.text: If I knew you, I'd cry.
jokes.setup: Monica sees how beautiful the bride looks in her dress
jokes.punchline: Her oddly conditional emotional response
jokes.explanation: Quirky line that captures Monica's emotional but practical personality.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you look absolutely beautiful!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, as long as you're wearing that dress.

jokes.text: Ball like a baby.
jokes.setup: Monica mispronounces 'bawl' as 'ball'
jokes.punchline: The malapropism creates an unintended image
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism humor. Monica's excited state leading to verbal fumble.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm going to ball my eyes out!

jokes.text: - I'm not. I just like to try these on. - I do the same thing. I'm just kidding. I'm getting married July 25. I'm just kidding too. I'm getting married in December.
jokes.setup: Megan admits she just likes trying on wedding dresses without being engaged
jokes.punchline: Both women reveal they were joking and are actually getting married, creating a competitive dynamic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that sets up the competitive dynamic between the two brides.

jokes.text: I almost called off my wedding.
jokes.setup: Discussion of a photographer who shows nude wedding photos of the best man
jokes.punchline: Monica's extreme reaction to seeing nude photos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's dramatic tendencies and perfectionist wedding planning.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! Oh my God! I can't unsee that! I can't unsee that!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why would you show me that?! Why would anyone take that?!

jokes.text: Is that spelled with a C or a K? It doesn't matter, they're both great!
jokes.setup: Discussion of the heavy metal band 'Carcass'
jokes.punchline: Megan's enthusiastic endorsement regardless of spelling, suggesting multiple equally terrible bands
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist joke that implies there are multiple terrible heavy metal bands with similar names.

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: I love Carcass! They're amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: Whether it's Carcass, Carcus, Carkass... they're all great bands!

jokes.text: I own this store.
jokes.setup: Megan loudly complains about how overpriced the store is
jokes.punchline: The store owner's simple, devastating revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with excellent timing. The owner's understated delivery makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: This place is way too expensive! How do you expect anyone to shop here with these prices?

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: Well, you're welcome to leave.

jokes.text: So does this come in another color, or...?
jokes.setup: Megan has just insulted the store in front of its owner
jokes.punchline: Her desperate attempt to change the subject and act like a normal customer
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the cringe moment, showing Megan's awkward attempt at recovery.

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: So... do you have these pants in a size 4?

dialogue.speaker: Store Owner
dialogue.line: You just said my store was a dump.

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: Right, but do you have them in a size 4?

jokes.text: Guy who was helping me had to leave because he hurt his back.
jokes.setup: Joey offers to help Kristen move, asking if she needs assistance
jokes.punchline: The previous helper injured himself, setting up Joey's physical inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Sets up Joey's upcoming physical comedy failure. Functional setup but not particularly funny on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'll help you move!

dialogue.speaker: Kristen
dialogue.line: Really? That would be great. My last helper threw out his back.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: His back? Oh, don't worry about me. I'm in great shape!

jokes.text: What a beautiful name. What is it again?
jokes.setup: Joey compliments Kristen on her beautiful name
jokes.punchline: He immediately admits he forgot what it was
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his superficial charm immediately undercut by his genuine dim-wittedness.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Kristen, that's such a beautiful name.

dialogue.speaker: Kristen
dialogue.line: Thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So... what is it?

jokes.text: Don't take a nap on this stoop. You could wake up with your shoes gone.
jokes.setup: Joey offers neighborhood advice to Kristen
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific warning about shoe theft while napping on stoops
jokes.explanation: Nicely absurd and specific advice that feels authentically Joey-like.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You gotta watch out for shoe thieves around here.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Especially if you're napping on the stoop. They can tell you're vulnerable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Last week, this guy took off his shoes to nap, and by the time he woke up, both shoes were gone.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: One shoe, maybe you could survive that. But two shoes? You're stuck.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So my advice: keep your shoes on, or at least tie them to your wrist.

jokes.text: You look strong. Why don't I take that, and you grab one of the boxes.
jokes.setup: Kristen suggests Joey take the lighter item while she takes the heavy boxes
jokes.punchline: She manipulates him by complimenting his strength while giving him the easier task
jokes.explanation: Smart reversal showing Kristen outsmarting Joey with flattery.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hey Joey, why don't you take the lighter boxes? You're so strong, you won't even notice them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really? You think I'm strong?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, totally. Here, take these feathers.

jokes.text: So when they open those doors, fan out.
jokes.setup: Rachel briefing Monica and herself for the wedding dress sale
jokes.punchline: Military-style tactical planning for a shopping trip
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of wedding dress shopping into military operation territory.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so we go in, we hit the clearance rack first, then we sweep through bridal, and we're out in twenty minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's a good plan, but what if they've already picked through the clearance?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Then we go to Plan B. We split up. You take menswear, I'll take the discount bin.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, why do I have menswear?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because you're faster. And if you see Chandler's ex, you know the signal.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What signal?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The bathroom trip. You fake a bathroom emergency and text me. We regroup in accessories.

jokes.text: Three sharp blasts. When you hear it, come running.
jokes.setup: Rachel's tactical briefing continues
jokes.punchline: Emergency whistle protocol for finding the dress
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the military metaphor with specific, absurd tactical details.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, if we get separated, use the emergency whistle protocol. Three short blasts means you found the dress, two long blasts means you found something almost as good, and one continuous blast means you've given up and gone to get coffee.

jokes.text: No, not it. Not it. Not it.
jokes.setup: Monica frantically searching through wedding dresses
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate rejections as she searches
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Monica's desperation. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, too white. No, too poufy. No, too... bridal.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What about this one? No, the neckline says 'desperate.' And this one? Definitely says 'I settled.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm running out of dresses and time! How is this happening?

jokes.text: Don't crowd me.
jokes.setup: Monica is in competitive shopping mode
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive territoriality over dress shopping space
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's competitive intensity.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Excuse me, this is MY rack. I was here first.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You need to move down at least three racks. I need my personal shopping space.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, don't touch that one! I already checked it and rejected it, so it's IN my pile.

jokes.text: You freak!
jokes.setup: Monica and Megan are fighting over the same dress
jokes.punchline: Their conflict escalates to direct insults
jokes.explanation: Basic escalation of conflict. Sets up the physical confrontation but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Maybe I do. I'm pretty feisty!
jokes.setup: Monica warns Megan she doesn't want to fight her
jokes.punchline: Megan's overconfident self-assessment as 'feisty'
jokes.explanation: Megan's delusional confidence before presumably getting dominated by Monica.

jokes.text: Okay! Hey? Oh, what do I do?!
jokes.setup: Rachel finds herself caught in the middle of Monica and Megan's dress fight
jokes.punchline: Her panicked helplessness when caught between two fighting women
jokes.explanation: Rachel as the unwilling mediator. Physical comedy moment but not particularly sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Stop! Monica, Megan, please just—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's MY dress!

dialogue.speaker: Megan
dialogue.line: No, it's mine!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, can we all just calm down and talk about this like adults?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh God, oh God, somebody help me!

jokes.text: You gotta hold my hand!
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe trying to navigate through the crazy shoppers to rescue Monica
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate need for physical support in the shopping chaos
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy continuation of the shopping chaos. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe! Phoebe, grab my hand!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm trying! There's a woman with a stroller the size of a Buick!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Don't let go of me! I can't feel my legs!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's probably just the circulation cut off from the crowd crushing you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, find Monica before I become a human pancake!

jokes.text: Did you tell her about the magical ride that starts with every flush?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions his date went well despite the sewage conversation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic reference to Ross's earlier inappropriate historical lesson
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reframes Ross's earlier inappropriate conversation. Chandler's sarcastic poetry elevates it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, nothing says romance like a good sewage story. That's right up there with your lecture on the history of the bidet.

jokes.text: I mean, that doofus is going to lose.
jokes.setup: Chandler reassures Joey he shouldn't worry about the other guy Kristen is dating
jokes.punchline: The 'other guy' is Ross, Chandler's friend, who he casually insults
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony as Chandler unknowingly insults Ross while trying to help Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Don't worry about the other guy. It's just Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ross?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, what's he gonna do? Bore her to death with some obscure fact about the mating habits of the Neanderthal?

jokes.text: Or I'm the one who dates her.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests he should date Kristen based on objective analysis
jokes.punchline: Joey's equally groundless counter-claim with no reasoning
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating non-argument between Ross and Joey. Joey's simplistic response is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You should date Kristen. I've done an objective analysis.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No way! She should date me. I've done an objective analysis too.

jokes.text: That's interesting. But check this out. I date her.
jokes.setup: Joey has just made his claim to date Kristen
jokes.punchline: Ross's pretentious setup followed by the exact same non-argument
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how both men are equally ridiculous. Ross's pseudo-intellectual framing of the same dumb argument is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, actually, if you examine the philological and sociological implications of dating within our immediate social circle, one could argue that the inherent power dynamics and potential for emotional disruption create a fundamentally untenable situation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No way, dude. I'm dating her.

jokes.text: And a slice... $6?
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross are trying to agree on a spending limit for their dates
jokes.punchline: Joey's suggestion is apparently so low it's absurd (a $6 pizza slice)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's financial limitations and simple tastes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How about we set a limit of six dollars?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Six dollars? Joey, that's a pizza slice!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Exactly.

jokes.text: Can I borrow $94?
jokes.setup: They agree on a $100 spending limit, which Joey seemed to accept
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately asks to borrow almost the entire amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - revealing he only had $6 to begin with. Great callback and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, can I borrow like ninety bucks?

jokes.text: You listen here, missy!
jokes.setup: Monica is on the phone, getting increasingly agitated
jokes.punchline: Her old-fashioned, aggressive phone manner
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's aggressive side with slightly old-fashioned phrasing that's amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hello? HELLO? Are you there? I can't hear you! Speak up! What? No, I'm not yelling, YOU'RE not speaking clearly enough! Listen, I don't have all day for this! Either you have something to say or you don't! Make up your mind! What do you want?!

jokes.text: Does that mean Carcass is available?
jokes.setup: Monica explains the dramatic wedding band situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical question about the terrible heavy metal alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - deadpan practicality in the face of drama, plus good callback to earlier joke.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, so what's the problem? Just get another band.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not that simple! They were perfect and now they're gone.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, but there are other bands out there. What about that heavy metal group that was playing at Central Perk last week?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, no.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? I thought they were great. That one song really spoke to me.

jokes.text: For me.
jokes.setup: Joey comments that Ross's outfit is good
jokes.punchline: Ross's correction revealing the outfit is supposed to benefit him, not make him look good
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment showing Ross's self-serving interpretation of fashion advice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, that's a great outfit!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Thanks! Actually, it's not supposed to make me look good. It's supposed to keep me warm.

jokes.text: What's this? Did you give yourself a facial? I have an oily T-zone.
jokes.setup: Joey notices something on Ross's face
jokes.punchline: Ross admits to skincare routines with technical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's vanity and Joey's bewilderment at male grooming.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, what's with your face? You're glowing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I've been using a retinol-based serum in the evenings and a hyaluronic acid moisturizer in the mornings.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Retinol? Hyaluronic? Dude, you sound like a Sephora commercial.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's called skincare, Joey. You should try it sometime.

jokes.text: I sent the flowers before the actual date, so technically... I didn't break any rules.
jokes.setup: Ross has sent expensive flowers, potentially breaking their spending agreement
jokes.punchline: His lawyerly rationalization about technicalities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross behavior - using technicalities and loopholes while pretending to be ethical.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, technically, the agreement said no gifts over fifty dollars. These flowers were forty-nine ninety-five.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I rounded down!

jokes.text: Joey got meat sauce on the banister again.
jokes.setup: Monica is cleaning while trying to avoid talking about swing music
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Joey's recurring messiness with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Minor character consistency joke about Joey's messy eating habits.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just going to organize these shelves and definitely not discuss swing music.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Monica, has anyone seen my... well, anything? I lost it somewhere in my apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, your apartment is like a black hole. Last week I found a sandwich from 1997 under your couch.

jokes.text: They suck so much that people die at their concerts. They just stop living.
jokes.setup: The gang is trying to convince Chandler that The Swing Kings are bad
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's extreme exaggeration about the band's awfulness
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe-style absurd exaggeration. The casual delivery of 'they just stop living' is perfectly deadpan.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, they are so bad! I mean, I've heard better music coming from a dying cat being dragged through a car wash!

jokes.text: you were the woman that I wanted to dance all my dances with.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why The Swing Kings are important to him
jokes.punchline: His unexpectedly romantic and sincere declaration
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that's funny because it's so unlike typical Chandler sarcasm. The sincerity is disarming.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The Swing Kings were the first band Monica and I ever listened to together. We were sitting on the couch in our apartment, and she put on their album, and I remember thinking, 'This is nice. This is really nice.' And then I realized it wasn't the music I loved. It was her.

jokes.text: Oh, crap.
jokes.setup: Chandler has just given a beautiful, romantic reason for wanting The Swing Kings
jokes.punchline: Monica's realization that she can't argue against this sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Chandler's romantic moment. Shows Monica's competitive nature defeated by genuine sentiment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I can't argue with that.

jokes.text: I guess the more muscles you have... the more they can spasm out of control.
jokes.setup: Ross is explaining his back problems to Kristen
jokes.punchline: His attempt to spin a weakness into evidence of his muscularity
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross pseudo-scientific rationalization to maintain his ego. Perfect character writing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know, I've been having some back problems lately. It's actually from carrying around all this muscle mass. My physique is so developed that my spine can barely support the weight of my own musculature.

dialogue.speaker: Kristen
dialogue.line: Ross, that's not how backs work.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, it's true! I read it on a forum. Very muscular men often have back issues. It's basically a sign of how jacked I am.

jokes.text: I don't anymore.
jokes.setup: Ross tells Joey he used to have a friend named Joey
jokes.punchline: His cold declaration that the friendship is over
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery showing Ross's passive-aggressive side.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I used to have a friend named Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Used to?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah. We're not friends anymore.

jokes.text: That's right. Don't you play a woman?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions Joey is on Days of Our Lives
jokes.punchline: Ross's dig about Joey playing a woman character
jokes.explanation: Ross's attempt to embarrass Joey backfires since Joey's character development makes this less embarrassing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, I didn't know you were on Days of Our Lives!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I play Dr. Drake Ramoray.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's great. And, uh, what does Dr. Drake Ramoray do?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He's a neurosurgeon.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh wow. And he's a woman?

jokes.text: A woman in a man's body. Much better.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to embarrass Joey about playing a woman
jokes.punchline: Joey's correction that actually makes his character sound more complex and interesting
jokes.explanation: Good turn-around where Joey's clarification actually makes his role sound more sophisticated.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You played a woman on Days of Our Lives?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I didn't play a woman. I played a man who was trapped in a woman's body.

jokes.text: another time after that.
jokes.setup: Ross has admitted to being married twice, which already sounds bad
jokes.punchline: His admission to a third marriage makes it even worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's romantic failures. The hesitant delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I've been married three times.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Three times?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I told you I was married twice!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you left out a wife!

jokes.text: Have you ever been so hungry on a date... that when the girl goes to the bathroom, you eat her food?
jokes.setup: Joey claims to be getting hungry
jokes.punchline: His very specific scenario about date food theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his food obsession creating very specific, embarrassing scenarios that he admits to.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm getting hungry.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what I hate? When you're on a date and the girl steals your food. So I'm like, 'Hey, I'm gonna get you back.' So I steal her food. But then she gets mad! 'Why'd you steal my food?' I'm like, 'Because you stole mine!' She's like, 'But I'm a girl.' I'm like, 'So what? That doesn't mean you get to steal my food just because you're a girl!' She's like, 'Well, you should've ordered something you didn't care about.' I'm like, 'Well, you should've ordered something you didn't care about!'

jokes.text: You said the waiter ate my crab cake.
jokes.setup: Joey has just revealed he ate date's food
jokes.punchline: Kristen revealing Joey has apparently done this to her and lied about it
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that Joey has done this exact thing to Kristen. Perfect comedic timing and structure.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I ate your food? I would never do that!

dialogue.speaker: Kristen
dialogue.line: Really? Because you ate my cheesecake last week and told me it was Monica.

jokes.text: Was it because the woman was straight or because she was a lesbian?
jokes.setup: Joey asks why Ross's first marriage ended
jokes.punchline: Joey's specific knowledge about Ross's ex-wife's sexuality reveals their friendship
jokes.explanation: Joey revealing he knows intimate details about Ross's life while pretending they're strangers.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why did your first marriage end?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, Carol realized she was a lesbian.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wow. So she's, uh... she's gay?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey. She's gay.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And you didn't know?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Huh. Well, that sucks.

jokes.text: Just seems like Ross is the kind of guy who'd marry a woman... on the verge of being a lesbian and then push her over the edge.
jokes.setup: Joey continues his fake analysis of Ross
jokes.punchline: His brutal psychological assessment suggesting Ross drives women to lesbianism
jokes.explanation: Brutal and funny psychological assessment. Joey's casual cruelty while maintaining the pretense of not knowing Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what your problem is? You drive women to lesbianism.

jokes.text: Were you on a poster for gonorrhea?
jokes.setup: The conversation has devolved into personal attacks
jokes.punchline: Ross's reference to Joey's embarrassing modeling work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to Joey's modeling career. The specificity of 'gonorrhea' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: At least I have a real job. You're still doing those ridiculous Calvin Klein underwear ads!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Those ads paid for my apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, they also paid for everyone in New York to see you in your underwear.

jokes.text: Have you ever slept in the same bed with a monkey?
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey are trading increasingly personal insults
jokes.punchline: Joey's reference to Ross's pet monkey Marcel
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the escalating personal attacks with callback to Ross's monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: At least I don't have a pet monkey that's smarter than me!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Marcel is not smarter than me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, Marcel learned sign language. What have you learned? How to use a microscope?

jokes.text: You leave Marcel out of this!
jokes.setup: Joey has insulted Ross's relationship with his monkey
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive reaction protecting his monkey's honor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's genuine affection for Marcel even in the middle of a fight.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey! Don't talk about Marcel like that! He's a sensitive monkey, okay? He has feelings!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ross, it's a monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So? That doesn't mean he doesn't understand what you're saying. He's very intelligent. He watches TV, he uses the bathroom—

jokes.text: Have you ever locked yourself in a TV cabinet, VD-boy?
jokes.setup: The insult battle continues
jokes.punchline: Ross's reference to both Joey's physical comedy mishaps and the gonorrhea poster
jokes.explanation: Good combination of physical comedy callback with the VD reference. Ross getting more vicious.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, at least I don't slip on banana peels for a living, and I've never had my face plastered on a 'Get Tested' poster!

jokes.text: Monkey-lover!
jokes.setup: The fight reaches its peak
jokes.punchline: Joey's final, childish insult about Ross and Marcel
jokes.explanation: Childish but effective final insult. The simplicity works after the escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well, you're a big... stupid... head!

jokes.text: Probably around gonorrhea.
jokes.setup: Joey asks when they lost Kristen during their fight
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact assessment of the exact moment their date went wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback. Ross's clinical assessment of their dating disaster is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: When she said 'I'm leaving you.'

jokes.text: Does a bear shit in the woods?
jokes.setup: Ross asks if Joey is hungry after their disastrous date
jokes.punchline: Joey's crude but enthusiastic confirmation using a folk saying
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey response - crude but honest. His priorities immediately return to food after romantic failure.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So, are you hungry?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, I'm so hungry I could eat a horse!

jokes.text: No. We only mess around at his place.
jokes.setup: Chandler jokes about Monica having another boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan response pretending to confirm the joke
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Chandler's joke. Monica playing along with the sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, Monica, another boyfriend? What's that, like your third one this week?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, at least I'm not making jokes about my own love life... oh wait, you don't have one.

jokes.text: Wow. You look hideous.
jokes.setup: Monica warns Chandler he has to hate the wedding dress
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clearly false assessment while obviously impressed
jokes.explanation: Chandler trying to follow Monica's instructions while clearly loving how she looks.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That dress is horrible. It's the worst thing I've ever seen. I hate it. Absolutely hate it.

jokes.text: That's like the most ugliest dress I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Chandler continues pretending to hate the dress
jokes.punchline: His grammatically incorrect attempt at extreme negativity
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt to be convincingly negative, undermined by his grammatical error showing he's not really trying.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I hate this dress more worser than I hate standing here talking about how much I hate this dress.

jokes.text: It makes me want to rip it off you.
jokes.setup: Chandler is supposedly describing how ugly the dress is
jokes.punchline: His 'insult' accidentally reveals his actual attraction
jokes.explanation: Good subversion where Chandler's attempt to insult the dress reveals his actual desire.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ugly? This dress is hideous! The way it clings to you, it's absolutely revolting. And those colors... they bring out your eyes in the most distracting way possible.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, that's not an insult.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know!

jokes.text: I should probably leave you girls alone.
jokes.setup: Chandler walks in on Ross and Joey doing face masks together
jokes.punchline: His assumption that they're being feminine
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with Chandler's deadpan reaction to the masculine grooming scene.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is this? A spa day? Did you guys run out of things to do?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's just a face mask, Chandler. It's good for your skin.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and a miniskirt is good for ventilation, but I'm not wearing one to the office.

jokes.text: Laugh all you want, but in 10 minutes, we'll have younger-looking skin.
jokes.setup: Chandler mocks Ross and Joey for their face masks
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive justification for their grooming routine
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's vanity and his attempt to make male grooming sound scientific.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, we're not metrosexuals. We're just secure enough in our masculinity to care about our appearance.

jokes.text: Oh, nice shot! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey are doing face masks
jokes.punchline: They're apparently also playing some kind of game or sport while in face masks
jokes.explanation: Visual gag ending showing the absurd combination of masculine competition with feminine beauty routines.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, so we just leave these on for ten minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, my dermatologist said it's great for the skin.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, you have a dermatologist?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, not officially. But I watch a lot of YouTube videos.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not... okay.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? Let's play ping pong.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, we have wet clay all over our faces.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So? Makes it more challenging.

tmdb_overview: Monica regrets telling a woman about a discount bridal shop when they end up fighting over the same dress. Meanwhile, Joey and Ross discover that they're dating the same girl.
insight: Cringe-heavy installment underperforms: 66 score despite solid 3.64 jokes-per-minute pace.
